title: quercus lanata subsp. leiocarpa
intro: Quercus lanata subsp. leiocarpa, also known as the rough-leaved or mountain white oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus lanata subsp. leiocarpa include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
